frameworks.name: context-dependent choice
frameworks.one_line: choices depend on the set of available rival options, not utilities alone

frameworks.name: decoy effects (attraction / similarity / compromise)
frameworks.one_line: three classic decoy biases unified as cases of a general repulsion principle

frameworks.name: value repulsion model
frameworks.one_line: attribute values are repulsed away from context provided by rivals

core_question: What is the full structure of decoy influence on two-attribute choice, and can a single mechanism explain the three classic biases?
key_assumptions: choice alternatives are described in a bidimensional attribute space (price, quality)
decoy influence can be parameterised as a function of decoy position relative to targets
individual variability in biases reflects parameters of a shared computation
manipulations: decoy stimulus position sampled exhaustively across two-attribute (price x quality) space
identity of the two preferred target items in incentive-compatible choices
individual participants as a large cohort
key_findings: decoy-influence map is highly structured beyond the three classic effects
a single simple model reproduces the full map and captures individual variability
attraction, similarity, and compromise effects emerge as special cases of one principle
the model predicts and validates a new interrelationship among the biases
interpretations: the three classic decoy biases share a common origin: repulsion of attribute values away from rival-option context
correlated biases across participants reflect parameters of one underlying context-repulsion computation
